title: Servers
content_html:
Servers

This section documents local server processes used by Studio, Analytics, Docs Viewer, UI Catalogue, public preview, and build tooling.

Use this section for:

  • local server architecture and boundaries
  • route namespace decisions
  • shared security rules for localhost APIs
  • the target shape for future Studio server requirements

Keep command-level usage in Scripts. Script docs should describe how to run a server, its flags, and its current endpoints. Server docs should describe why the server boundary exists and how the pieces should evolve.

Read this section in this order:

  1. Local Studio Server Architecture

Current local server scripts:

  • Local Studio App for the Studio app server boundary and server module ownership
  • Local Studio Routes for Studio shell routes, route-local body modules, retired routes, and route checks
  • Local Studio APIs for Catalogue, audit, and risk endpoint groups
  • bin/local-analytics for Analytics tag routes/APIs and Data Sharing routes/APIs
  • bin/local-ui-catalogue for isolated UI Catalogue demos
  • docs-viewer/bin/docs-viewer for Docs Viewer /docs/ manage mode, generated reads, and docs management APIs
  • bin/public-site-preview for optional public Jekyll preview
  • Catalogue Write Services for catalogue API service modules hosted by the Local Studio app

Risk operations do not have a separate server. Studio Risk Operations records the decision that risk dashboards, app inventories, audit launching, unified activity review, and risk-related local artifacts use the Local Studio app server.
